stages.name: Guided Recognition
stages.description: Critical signals are highlighted or hinted. Focus on learning the patterns.

stages.name: Unguided Detection
stages.description: No hints provided. You must rely on your clinical judgment.

stages.name: Pressured Performance
stages.description: Time is limited. Distinguish signals from noise under pressure.

stages.name: Expert Calibration
stages.description: Assess your confidence. Overconfidence in noise is as dangerous as missing signals.

adaptiveRules.repeatDrill: If score < 70% or critical signal missed

drills.scenario: A 62-year-old male with a history of hypertension and smoking presents to the ED with 'indigestion' that started 2 hours ago while gardening.

findings.text: Substernal pressure radiating to left jaw
findings.explanation: Classic anginal distribution reflecting myocardial ischemia.
Reflects sympathetic nervous system activation due to cardiogenic stress.
Indicates decreased ventricular compliance during acute ischemia.
Direct evidence of transmural injury in the anterior wall.

findings.text: Sharp pain that worsens with deep inspiration
findings.explanation: Suggests pleuritic pain or pericarditis rather than typical MI.
Points toward GERD, though MI can sometimes mimic GI distress.

findings.text: Tenderness to palpation over the sternum
findings.explanation: Suggests musculoskeletal costochondritis rather than visceral cardiac pain.

findings.text: Recent minor bruise on the right shin

drills.scenario: A 74-year-old female is brought in by her daughter who noticed her mother was 'acting strange' during breakfast.

findings.explanation: Indicates upper motor neuron involvement in the contralateral hemisphere.
Suggests involvement of Broca's area in the dominant hemisphere.

findings.text: Extensor plantar response (Babinski sign) on the right
findings.explanation: Objective sign of corticospinal tract dysfunction.

findings.text: Loss of peripheral vision in the right visual field
findings.explanation: Homonymous hemianopsia indicates post-chiasmatic visual pathway lesion.

findings.text: Gradual onset of confusion over 3 days
findings.explanation: More characteristic of metabolic encephalopathy or infection than acute stroke.

findings.text: Bilateral hand tremors that improve with alcohol
findings.explanation: Suggests essential tremor rather than acute focal neurological deficit.
Common benign condition that can distract from acute focal signs.

findings.text: Uses a specific brand of lavender soap
Has a collection of antique thimbles
Mild osteoarthritis in the left knee

drills.scenario: A 19-year-old college student presents with abdominal pain that started around the umbilicus and moved to the lower right side.

findings.explanation: Classic anatomical location for inflamed appendix irritation of the peritoneum.
Indicates peritoneal inflammation, a hallmark of advanced appendicitis.
Referred pain to RLQ when LLQ is palpated suggests peritoneal irritation.
Systemic markers of acute inflammatory/infectious process.

findings.text: Pain that radiates to the groin and scrotum
findings.explanation: More characteristic of nephrolithiasis (kidney stones) than appendicitis.

findings.text: History of multiple episodes of similar pain
findings.explanation: Suggests chronic condition like IBD or IBS rather than acute appendicitis.

findings.text: Pain significantly improved after a large bowel movement
findings.explanation: Points toward functional bowel issues or constipation.

findings.text: Has a small tattoo on the left ankle

drills.scenario: A 45-year-old woman presents with sudden shortness of breath after returning from a 14-hour flight.

findings.explanation: Reflects pulmonary infarction or pleural irritation from the embolus.
Compensatory mechanisms for acute ventilation-perfusion mismatch.
Suggests DVT as the source of the pulmonary embolus.

findings.text: Hypoxemia with a widened A-a gradient
findings.explanation: Physiological evidence of impaired gas exchange due to vascular obstruction.

findings.text: Productive cough with thick green sputum
findings.explanation: Suggests pneumonia or bronchitis rather than acute PE.

findings.text: Pain that is reproducible by pressing on the ribs
findings.explanation: Points toward musculoskeletal chest wall pain.
Can confuse the diagnosis of acute dyspnea but usually has wheezing.

findings.text: Has a slight tan from a recent vacation
Recent minor paper cut on index finger

drills.scenario: An 82-year-old nursing home resident is brought in with increased confusion and a new cough.

findings.text: Dullness to percussion in the right base
findings.explanation: Indicates consolidation of lung tissue by inflammatory fluid.

findings.text: Bronchial breath sounds in the periphery
findings.explanation: Reflects sound transmission through consolidated lung.

findings.text: Inspiratory crackles (rales) that do not clear with cough
findings.explanation: Sign of fluid or exudate in the alveolar spaces.
Evidence of impaired gas exchange due to alveolar filling.
More characteristic of COPD or asthma than focal pneumonia.
Suggests heart failure or fluid overload rather than primary pneumonia.
Can mask the new, acute change in cough quality.

findings.text: Has a photo of grandchildren in her purse
Mild hearing loss in the right ear
Has a small scar from a 1950s appendectomy

drills.scenario: A 22-year-old graduate student is brought to the ED by roommates because he is 'unusually sleepy' and has a severe headache.

findings.explanation: Reflects meningeal irritation and protective muscle spasm.
Common symptom of meningeal inflammation affecting cranial nerves.

findings.text: Non-blanching petechial rash on the trunk
findings.explanation: Highly suggestive of meningococcemia and vasculitis.
Involuntary hip flexion when neck is flexed indicates severe meningeal irritation.
Can lead to delay in seeking care for a 'different' kind of headache.

findings.text: Rhinorrhea and sore throat for 2 days
findings.explanation: Suggests common viral URI, which can precede but is not diagnostic of meningitis.
Can occur in optic neuritis or simple viral syndromes, less specific than photophobia.

findings.text: Backpack contains a laptop and textbooks
Recent trip to a local coffee shop
Has a slight stutter when nervous

drills.scenario: A 55-year-old male presents with swelling and pain in his left leg that started yesterday after a long day of driving.

findings.text: Unilateral pitting edema of the left ankle
findings.explanation: Reflects venous outflow obstruction and increased hydrostatic pressure.

findings.text: Calf circumference 3cm larger than the right side
findings.explanation: Objective measure of significant unilateral swelling.

findings.text: Tenderness along the deep venous system
findings.explanation: Direct sign of inflammation/thrombosis in the vein.
Reflects shunting of blood due to deep venous obstruction.

findings.text: Bilateral leg swelling that improves with elevation
findings.explanation: More characteristic of chronic venous insufficiency or heart failure.

findings.text: Sharp pain in the calf when walking (claudication)
findings.explanation: Suggests peripheral arterial disease rather than venous thrombosis.

findings.text: History of a recent minor ankle sprain
findings.explanation: Can provide a false 'alternative' explanation for unilateral pain.

findings.text: Has a small mole on the right shoulder

drills.scenario: A 68-year-old male with a history of diabetes is brought in by EMS with altered mental status and a fever.

findings.explanation: Indicates critical hypotension and impaired organ perfusion.
Marker of anaerobic metabolism and tissue dysoxia.
Sign of acute kidney injury due to hypoperfusion.
Clinical sign of poor peripheral microcirculatory perfusion.

findings.text: Isolated fever of 38.5°C without other symptoms
findings.explanation: Fever alone is common and does not define sepsis without organ dysfunction.
Common in diabetics and can distract from acute metabolic derangement.
May be the source, but the focus should be on the systemic response (sepsis).

findings.text: Has a pair of bifocals in his pocket
Wears a medical alert bracelet for diabetes
Has a small scar on his forehead
Prefers to sleep on his left side

drills.scenario: A 16-year-old female with Type 1 Diabetes presents with nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

findings.explanation: Compensatory respiratory alkalosis for profound metabolic acidosis.
Direct clinical sign of acetone production from ketosis.
Objective evidence of significant metabolic acidosis.

findings.text: Strongly positive ketones in the urine
findings.explanation: Confirms the presence of ketosis, a defining feature of DKA.
DKA usually presents with much higher glucose (>250), though euglycemic DKA exists.
Can be a trigger for DKA but can also be a false alternative diagnosis for vomiting.
Common in DKA but can lead to unnecessary surgical consultation.

findings.text: Has a smartphone with a cracked screen
Uses a specific brand of lip balm

drills.scenario: A 30-year-old male is brought to the ED after eating at a Thai restaurant. He is complaining of throat tightness.

findings.explanation: Indicates critical upper airway edema and impending obstruction.
Classic cutaneous manifestations of systemic IgE-mediated reaction.
Reflects lower airway bronchoconstriction.
Indicates anaphylactic shock due to massive vasodilation.

findings.text: Isolated itchy throat without visible swelling
findings.explanation: Common in mild oral allergy syndrome, not necessarily anaphylaxis.

findings.text: History of mild seasonal hay fever
findings.explanation: Atopic history is relevant but not diagnostic of the acute event.

findings.text: Nausea and one episode of loose stool
findings.explanation: Can be part of anaphylaxis but also common in simple food poisoning.

findings.text: Has a wallet with three credit cards

drills.scenario: A 72-year-old male presents with worsening shortness of breath and the need to sleep on three pillows.

findings.explanation: Highly specific symptom of left ventricular dysfunction and pulmonary congestion.
Sign of rapid ventricular filling into a dilated, non-compliant ventricle.

findings.text: Bilateral pulmonary crackles that don't clear with cough
findings.explanation: Reflects alveolar edema from high pulmonary venous pressures.
Direct clinical measure of elevated right atrial pressure.
Can coexist and make the cause of dyspnea difficult to distinguish.

findings.text: Mild productive cough with white phlegm
findings.explanation: Common in HF ('cardiac asthma') but can be mistaken for simple bronchitis.

findings.text: Trace ankle edema at the end of the day
findings.explanation: Very non-specific; can occur in healthy elderly or due to medications.

findings.text: Has a set of car keys in his hand
Has a small age spot on his left hand

drills.scenario: An 85-year-old female is brought from her assisted living facility because she is 'not herself' and has been falling.

findings.explanation: Common presentation of UTI in the elderly due to bladder irritability.
Reflects inflammation of the bladder wall (cystitis).

findings.text: Positive leukocyte esterase and nitrites on dipstick
findings.explanation: Biochemical evidence of bacteriuria and pyuria.

findings.text: Acute worsening of baseline cognitive function
findings.explanation: Delirium is a frequent systemic manifestation of infection in the elderly.
Common in elderly and can lead to over-diagnosis of UTI.
Can mask acute delirium if not carefully compared to baseline.

findings.text: Dark, concentrated urine with a strong odor
findings.explanation: More often a sign of dehydration than infection.

findings.text: Has a small bottle of water in her walker bag
Wears a hearing aid in the left ear
Recent meal was chicken and rice
